subject: Cloud Computing
title: GDPR and Cloud Data
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on GDPR and Cloud Data.

hook.text: A European user's data is stored on a U.S. server. Under GDPR, that might be illegal.

buildup.text: GDPR requires companies to protect EU personal data and limits where it can be stored and processed.

discovery.text: Cloud providers offer EU-only regions. Data residency controls ensure data never leaves European borders.

twist.text: GDPR fines are massive: up to 4% of global revenue. Meta was fined $1.3 billion for data transfer violations.

climax.text: Cloud and GDPR coexist, but it requires careful architecture: region selection, encryption, and consent management.

punchline.text: GDPR doesn't ban the cloud. It demands you use it responsibly.

quiz.question: How do cloud providers help customers comply with GDPR?
options: By offering EU-only regions with data residency controls
By automatically deleting all EU user data after 30 days
By making all data publicly accessible for transparency
